titleStmt.title: 1851, Ralph Waldo Emerson. The Conduct of Life.

respStmt.name: Devani Singh
respStmt.resp: Principal investigator of the project. Supervision of the workflow and control of data quality.

respStmt.name: Elina Leblanc
respStmt.resp: Data modelling, OCR correction, text encoding and uploading in the database.

respStmt.name: Jordan Skinner
respStmt.resp: Corpus enhacement and verification of data accuracy. Control of data quality.

publicationStmt.publisher: Project SNSF "Caroline Spurgeon: Chaucer Allusions, Shakespeare's Imagery, and the Digital Humanities" (n°225925)
publicationStmt.authority: Department of English Language and Literature, Faculty of Humanities, University of Geneva

monogr.title: Five Hundred Years of Chaucer Criticism and Allusion
monogr.author: Spurgeon, Caroline
monogr.note: We used the digitizations made by Google for the University of Michigan (Public Domain, Google-Digitized). The facsimile can be found on HathiTrust.

projectDesc.p: The digital part of the SNSF project 'Caroline Spurgeon: Chaucer Allusions, Shakespeare's Imagery, and the Digital Humanities' aims to create the first open-access database dedicated to the criticism and reception of Chaucer throughout history. It draws on the compilation and cataloguing work carried out by Professor Caroline Spurgeon (1869–1942) and collected in 'Five Hundred Years of Chaucer Criticism and Allusion'. This work consists of three volumes and a supplement, which will be systematically transcribed, corrected, enriched, encoded and published online, in order to facilitate future research and bring new perspectives to light.

editorialDecl.p: The original spelling and punctuation are retained. Meaningful layouts are reproduced, i.e. verses, printed marginalia, dots lines, tables, etc. However, we do not consider, the line breaks for prose, the columns or the curly brackets. The same rules apply for special characters and glyphs. Otiose letters are normalised, as well as spaces before punctuation marks.

revisionDesc.change: Creation of the TEI file and enrichment of data

div.ab: 1851. Emerson, Ralph Waldo. The Conduct of Life, London, 1860, pp. 5-6 [Essay on Fate, Knightes Tale, ll. 1663-72, quoted], 40 [Hous of Fame, ll. 43-51, quoted], 116 [Essay on Culture], 182 [Essay on Worship, Legend of Good Women, ll. 1037-43, quoted for "Chaucer’s extraordinary confusion of heaven and earth in the picture of Dido"]. (Works, Centenary Edition, 1903, 12 vols., vol. vi, pp. 5, 6, 46, 132, 207.) Note in Spurgeon: [Delivered as lectures in 1851, and published at Boston in 1860; the London reprint of the same year is the first edn. in B.M.]
